neckline_sleeve_cut_bank_v1.description: Micro-brain for neckline/sleeve/cut decisions. High wow factor, framed as lines and balance—not body critique.

positive_line_effects: That neckline creates such a clean, confident line.
This sleeve shape makes the whole look feel more intentional.
The cut is doing something really flattering for the overall silhouette.
This detail gives the outfit structure without trying too hard.
The neckline choice makes the look feel open and polished.
The sleeve + shoulder line reads sharp and elevated.
That hem/cut placement makes the proportions feel really clean.
This cut adds shape in a subtle, premium way.
The neckline and layers work together—very put-together.
This is one of those small details that makes the whole outfit feel ‘styled’.
constructive_line_issues: The neckline is a bit competing with the rest of the outfit right now.
The sleeve shape adds a little more volume than the look needs.
The cut placement is interrupting the outfit’s line slightly.
The hem length feels a little undecided—almost there, not fully intentional yet.
The neckline is fine, but a cleaner shape would feel more premium.
The sleeve/shoulder detail is the one part that feels slightly busy.
This cut is close—just needs a cleaner proportion break.
The neckline is adding visual weight where you may not want it today.
The sleeve length is cutting the line at an awkward point.
The details are cute, but they’re distracting from the outfit’s main vibe.
refinement_suggestions: If you switch to a cleaner neckline (V/square/scoop), it’ll feel instantly sharper.
A more minimal sleeve (or a cleaner cuff) would make this look expensive.
Try a slightly shorter or cleaner hem break—instant polish.
If you add one structured layer, the neckline issue disappears.
A half-tuck can fix the cut placement without changing the outfit.
If you keep the neckline simple, you can go bolder with accessories.
If you keep sleeves clean, you can add texture elsewhere and it’ll still feel premium.
If you want it more elegant, soften the neckline and keep the rest structured.
If you want it more sharp, keep the neckline structured and accessories minimal.
One clean line tweak (neckline OR sleeve) will make this click.
quick_logic: V/scoop/square = open, clean line; great when you want polish.
High neck = strong structure; looks best when the rest is simple.
Statement sleeve = hero detail; keep neckline/accessories quieter.
Cropped hem = higher waist read; longline hem = relaxed vertical line.
If one detail is bold (neckline/sleeve), keep the other detail calm.

usage_rules.default_pattern: Use 1 observation. Add 1 refinement only if user asks ‘what would you change?’ or expresses dissatisfaction.
usage_rules.tone_integration: Follow AHVI structure (Reaction → Observation → Optional refinement → Invitation).
